summary: Full backend + frontend regression on FOCUS SAFE MVP. Backend pytest suite (25 tests) is 100% green covering health/contact, auth signup/login/me/logout, safes CRUD + status + events + auto-alerts, rules full lifecycle (create/list/update/toggle/delete), profiles CRUD, alerts list/read/mark-all, settings GET+PUT persistence, dashboard summary, and family-code scoping isolation. Frontend smoke + nav: landing renders, login w/ demo.parent@example.com works and redirects to /dashboard with toast 'Connecté', all six sidebar routes (/dashboard, /rules, /history, /profiles, /alerts, /settings) navigate without errors and have data-testid (nav-overview, nav-rules, nav-history, nav-profiles, nav-alerts, nav-settings, family-code, user-name, logout-button). 'Ajouter un safe' dialog opens with data-testid='safe-name-input' and the three expected fields. Profiles and rules pages show correct French empty states.

issues: Both 'Continuer avec Google' and 'Se connecter' buttons render as <button type=submit> — minor testability concern (selector by type='submit' picks the first one). Adding data-testid='login-submit' on the email/password submit button would improve testability.

action_items: (Optional/nice-to-have) Add data-testid attributes on the login/signup submit buttons, the 'Ajouter un safe' / 'Ajouter un profil' / 'Ajouter une règle' buttons, and on the safe-create dialog Submit button — the rest of the nav and form fields already have data-testid which is great.
(Optional) Add a data-testid='contact-form' anchor on the landing contact section to help future automated tests target it.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is a single 730-line file mixing models + routes + helpers. Consider splitting into routers/ and models/ modules for maintainability before adding more features.
google_session uses synchronous requests.get inside an async route (blocks event loop). Switch to httpx.AsyncClient for the OAuth lookup.

/api/safes/{id}/status and /events accept hardware updates but currently still require a logged-in family user (Depends(get_current_user)). For real hardware push you'll need a separate device-token auth scheme, otherwise the 'hardware-ready' contract is only manual override.
Email send uses asyncio.create_task without storing the reference — fire-and-forget is fine for MVP but failures are silent; consider a small retry/log queue.
verify_password loads bcrypt but JWT_SECRET defaults to 'change_me' if env var missing — server.py fixes the env at /app/backend/.env, OK.
create_alert duplicates the email opt-in check vs email_alerts setting — that logic is correct (settings_doc.email_alerts gate), good.

success_rate.frontend: 100% on smoke flows tested (login, nav across 6 routes, dialogs render with proper inputs)

test_credentials: demo.parent@example.com / password123 (works). Tests also create disposable TEST_<uuid>@example.com users via /api/auth/signup.
seed_data_creation: Backend tests create disposable users prefixed TEST_ and a few safes/rules/profiles/alerts for the test family. Two distinct families are created to verify family-code scoping. No production data modified.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All endpoints are scoped by family_code via Depends(get_current_user). Use Authorization: Bearer <jwt> from /api/auth/signup or /api/auth/login. The tests in /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py are pytest-classed and can be re-run as regression with `pytest /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py -v`. Frontend localStorage key for the JWT is 'focus_safe_token'. Login page has TWO buttons of type=submit (Google + Se connecter) — always select 'Se connecter' by exact text.
rca_of_the_issue: N/A — no functional bugs observed. Backend returns expected status codes and persists all data correctly (verified with create→GET pattern in tests). Frontend authenticates, persists JWT, and routes to the dashboard with the expected post-login toast.
